title: Ensure that the --peer-auto-tls argument is not set to true

input_row.title: Ensure that the --peer-auto-tls argument is not set to true
input_row.description: Do not use automatically generated self-signed certificates for TLS connections between peers.
input_row.rationale: etcd is a highly-available key value store used by Kubernetes deployments for persistent storage of all of its REST API objects. These objects are sensitive in nature and should be accessible only by authenticated etcd peers in the etcd cluster. Hence, do not use self-signed certificates for authentication.
input_row.audit: Run the following command on the etcd server node: ps -ef | grep etcd Verify that if the --peer-auto-tls argument exists, it is not set to true . Note: This recommendation is applicable only for etcd clusters. If you are using only one etcd server in your environment then this recommendation is not applicable.
input_row.remediation: Edit the etcd environment file (for example, /etc/etcd/etcd.conf ) on the etcd server node and comment out the ETCD_PEER_AUTO_TLS parameter: #ETCD_PEER_AUTO_TLS="true" Edit the etcd startup file (for example, /etc/systemd/system/multi- user.target.wants/etcd.service ) and remove the startup parameter for --peer-auto- tls . Based on your system, reload the daemon and restart the etcd service. For example, systemctl daemon-reload systemctl restart etcd.service Impact: All peers attempting to communicate with the etcd server will require a valid client certificate for authentication. 123 | P a g e
input_row.default_value: Note: This recommendation is applicable only for etcd clusters. If you are using only one etcd server in your environment then this recommendation is not applicable. By default, --peer-auto-tls argument is set to false .

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Manual

**MANUAL STEPS:**
1. SSH into the etcd server node.
2. Run the command: `ps -ef | grep etcd`
3. Look for the `--peer-auto-tls` argument in the output.
4. Verify that if the `--peer-auto-tls` argument exists, it is not set to `true`.
5. If the argument is found and set to `true`, proceed to edit the etcd configuration.
6. Edit the etcd environment file (e.g., `/etc/etcd/etcd.conf`) and comment out the `ETCD_PEER_AUTO_TLS` parameter: `#ETCD_PEER_AUTO_TLS="true"`.
7. Edit the etcd startup file (e.g., `/etc/systemd/system/multi-user.target.wants/etcd.service`) and remove the startup parameter for `--peer-auto-tls`.
8. Reload the daemon and restart the etcd service using the commands:
   - `systemctl daemon-reload`
   - `systemctl restart etcd.service`
9. Confirm that all peers attempting to communicate with the etcd server now require a valid client certificate for authentication.
